visa_policy.notes: Admission and transit refused to nationals of Israel; passports stamped with Israeli visa or seal also refused at Lebanese border. Visa fee for most categories: 2,000,000 LBP. Lebanon does not operate an e-visa or ETA system; visas are issued at border/airport or through Lebanese embassies abroad. Ivory Coast, Ghana, and Nigeria require prior General Security authorization and are classified under conditional_access rather than visa_on_arrival. Worker-exporting countries (Bangladesh, Philippines, India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, etc.) require prior authorization from General Security for work-related entry.

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must be a businessman, banker, director, CEO, doctor, engineer, or lawyer; valid GCC residency permit required; visa granted at airport or border post for 11 months; visa validity cannot exceed passport or residence permit validity; spouses and children without their own GCC residency follow standard nationality-based rules.

credential.subtype: Lebanese national identity card, expired Lebanese passport, or Lebanese civil document

conditional_access.conditions: Travelers of Lebanese origin holding any foreign passport, together with a valid Lebanese national identity card, an expired Lebanese passport, or a Lebanese civil document, are exempted from visa requirements and admitted as citizens of the Republic of Lebanon.
Holders of Armenian diplomatic, official, service, or special passports are granted free automatic entry; maximum stay 180 days per calendar year.
Holders of Brazilian or Cuban diplomatic, official, service, or special passports are granted free automatic entry; maximum stay 3 months per calendar year.
Holders of Colombian diplomatic, official, or service passports are granted free automatic entry; maximum stay 3 months, renewable once within one year.
Holders of Cypriot or Venezuelan diplomatic, official, or service passports are granted free automatic entry; maximum stay 90 days.
Holders of Greek or Kyrgyz diplomatic, official, service, or special passports are granted free automatic entry; maximum stay 3 months within any 6-month period.
Holders of diplomatic passports ONLY (not service or official) from Hungary, Malta, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland are granted free automatic entry; maximum stay 90 days.

Holders of Kazakhstani diplomatic, official, service, or special passports NOT assigned to a diplomatic mission or consular office are granted free direct entry visas at all border crossings; valid for 1 month within any 180-day period.
All countries' diplomats, UN personnel, and DCAF workers granted multiple-entry free visa of 6 months at airport/border.

Prior authorization from Lebanese General Security is required before travel. Once approved, a visa on arrival (1 month, extendable to 3 months) is issued at the airport. Conditions: two-way non-refundable ticket, hotel reservation or residential address, and USD 2,000 cash equivalent. Female citizens of Ghana and Ivory Coast require additional prior authorization for tourism. Nigeria requires General Security pre-approval for all travelers.

cbi.notes: Lebanon does not operate a formal, codified citizenship-by-investment program. Investor naturalizations may occur through discretionary presidential/cabinet powers under Decree 15/1925 but there is no official structured CBI program with published criteria or regular intake. No official government source advertises a CBI program.

programs.name: Investor Residency (Annual Residence)
programs.notes: Requires a blocked bank account of at least 150,000,000 LBP at a Lebanese bank (statement covering at least 3 months, bank-signed). Also requires lease contract or title deed, investment contracts, commercial register extract, and professional license. Annual residence permit with mandatory health insurance for foreigners. A 'permanent residence' category exists on the General Security site but its eligibility criteria are not published online. No confirmed path from investor residency to permanent residence or citizenship found in official sources.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Nigeria, Ghana, and Ivory Coast moved from visa_policy.visa_on_arrival to conditional_access (basis=special_status) because Lebanese General Security explicitly requires prior General Security authorization from these nationalities before travel - they are NOT unconditional visa-on-arrival. This is a classification correction, not a policy change.

CORRECTION: Notes wording for 'female artists/masseuses' restriction clarified in all visa_on_arrival entries to specify 'classified as artists or masseuses' rather than the vague 'if classified as artists/masseuses' - this restriction does NOT apply to all female travelers from those nations, only those in those occupational categories.
CORRECTION: Jordan entry note updated to specify 'Jordanian nationals with national numbers' as the official page confirms this qualifier for tourism entry.
CORRECTION: Iraq entry note revised to align with official language: traders require chamber of commerce registration; doctors/engineers require professional board certification.
CORRECTION: Arab-states visa-on-arrival note updated to use 'two-way non-refundable ticket' (official language) instead of 'return ticket'.
CONFIRMED: Lebanon has no e-visa or ETA system - verified against general-security.gov.lb as of June 2026.
CONFIRMED: RBI minimum of 150,000,000 LBP in blocked account - verified against general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/171.
CONFIRMED: No CBI program - no official source found advertising a structured program.
CONFIRMED: Admission refused to Israeli passport holders and passports stamped with Israeli visas - confirmed via Lebanon Embassy UK source.
ADDED: lebanon-embassy.org (UK Embassy) to official_domains as it provided corroborating official entry policy.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: All visa_free (GCC + Jordan) and visa_on_arrival nationality lists re-confirmed against general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/38. No additions or removals detected. The official page uses 'exempted from entry visas' language for GCC and Jordanian passport-with-national-number holders, confirming visa_free classification. Turkey confirmed as visa_on_arrival (cost-free visa delivered at the airport/border, not an exemption). All Arab conditional-VoA countries (Algeria, Djibouti, Egypt, Comoros, Libya, Morocco, Mauritania, Somalia, Sudan, Tunisia, Yemen) re-confirmed with $2,000 USD + round-trip ticket + hotel reservation conditions.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Syria NOT included in any visa_policy list. Official page places Syria under 'prior authorization required' (Article Ninth). Wikipedia's claim that Syria has conditional VoA is not supported by the Lebanese General Security official page - Syria is excluded from all visa_policy lists and not added to conditional_access because precise tourist-entry terms are not articulated on the official page.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Seychelles NOT included in visa_free or visa_on_arrival. Wikipedia notes a visa-free agreement was 'signed but not ratified'; the official General Security page lists Seychelles only under the worker-exporting countries requiring prior authorization. No ratified visa-free agreement confirmed - entry not added.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: RBI minimum of 150,000,000 LBP in blocked account re-confirmed against general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/171. Path to permanent residency remains case-by-case discretionary; no publicly published eligibility criteria.
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: No CBI program - re-confirmed, no official source advertising a structured program found.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Full re-fetch of general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/38 confirms all visa_free (GCC + Jordan), all visa_on_arrival nationality lists, and all conditional_access entries remain unchanged. Turkey confirmed as visa_on_arrival (cost-free multi-entry 3-month visa at airport/border). Seychelles still listed only under worker-export prior-authorization category, not visa-free - confirmed not added. Syria still not referenced as tourist-entry eligible on official page - confirmed not added. No e-visa or ETA system found. RBI 150,000,000 LBP minimum re-confirmed via general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/171. MFA page (mfa.gov.lb) confirmed to not enumerate visa-free nationalities. No policy changes detected; record is current as of 2026-06-29.
gaps: Lebanon does not operate an e-visa or ETA system - confirmed from official General Security site.
No official digital nomad or skilled-worker fast-track program found on any official Lebanese government source.
The 150,000,000 LBP RBI minimum is subject to exchange rate fluctuations; official page states the figure verbatim in LBP.

Path to permanent residency or citizenship through investor residency is not specified on the official General Security page. A 'permanent residence' category exists on the site but eligibility criteria are not published.
Lebanon's Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (mfa.gov.lb) does not enumerate visa-free nationalities - General Security is the primary official immigration authority.
CBI: No official government program found; discretionary naturalizations possible under Decree 15/1925 but no structured program.
Freedom-of-movement agreements: Lebanon is not a member of GCC, EU, ASEAN, or any common travel area.
No official Lebanese government source confirms credential-based access for holders of valid US, Schengen, UK, Canadian, or Australian visas/residence permits - not recorded.
Indian OCI cardholder entry: No provision found on any official Lebanese government source - not recorded.
Bilateral diplomatic passport waiver agreement dates not published on general-security.gov.lb for any of the countries listed; exact agreement dates and formal treaty names remain unconfirmed.
Lebanese-origin diaspora rule confirmed via general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/38 reference but verbatim text not machine-extracted; recorded with source_official=true based on official page content.

Benin, Cameroon, Ethiopia, Kenya, Liberia, Mali, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, Philippines, Indonesia, Sierra Leone nationalities also require prior General Security authorization per official sources; these are work-visa-related restrictions and not included in visa_policy or conditional_access as their tourist-entry classification remains ambiguous from the official page alone.

Syria: Official General Security page references prior authorization requirement (Article Ninth) for Syrian nationals but does not articulate clear tourist-entry terms. Syria is not added to any visa_policy list or conditional_access pending confirmation of exact terms from an official Lebanese source.

Seychelles: Wikipedia notes a Lebanon-Seychelles visa-free agreement was signed but not yet ratified as of 2026. The official General Security page only lists Seychelles under worker-exporting countries requiring prior authorization. Seychelles NOT added to visa_free until ratification is confirmed on an official Lebanese government source.

visa_types.name: Tourist / Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Lebanon for tourism, leisure, or personal visits. Issued at airport/border for eligible nationalities or through Lebanese embassies abroad.
visa_types.notes: Free for Section One countries (USA, UK, EU, Australia, Japan, Canada, etc.) and certain Arab nationals; extendable to 3 months at no cost. Arab nationals from Algeria, Egypt, Morocco, Tunisia, Libya, etc. require round-trip ticket, hotel reservation, and USD 2,000 equivalent. Standard fee of 2,000,000 LBP applies where charged. Israel passport holders and those with Israeli stamps are refused entry.

visa_types.name: Collective Tourist Group Visa
visa_types.purpose: For organized tourist groups of 8 or more members travelling together to Lebanon.
visa_types.notes: Groups from visa-exempt countries pay no fee. Nationals from Nigeria, Ghana, and Ivory Coast in tourist groups are charged 2,450,000 LBP per group member. Group must travel together and depart together. Prior authorization from General Security required for nationalities that need pre-clearance.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For travelers passing through Lebanon en route to a third country without intending to stay.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct visa category (single or double entry) at a fee of 2,000,000 LBP. Issued through Lebanese embassies or at border for eligible nationalities. Israel passport holders and those with Israeli stamps refused. Entry/transit conditions mirror tourist visa nationality tiers.

visa_types.name: Business / Commercial Visa
visa_types.purpose: For investors, businessmen, traders, and professionals entering Lebanon for commercial consultations, training, or business activities.
visa_types.notes: Investors and businessmen with accreditation from IDAL (Investment Development Authority of Lebanon) or an official invitation from a Lebanese business entity may receive a 6-month visa. Traders from certain Arab countries (Iraq, Yemen, etc.) with chamber of commerce registration, and doctors/engineers with professional board certification, may be auto-issued a visa on arrival. Fee: 2,000,000 LBP where applicable.

visa_types.name: Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals authorized to work in Lebanon under a contract approved by the Ministry of Labor.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior authorization from General Security and Ministry of Labor before entry. Valid for 3 months, renewable once (total maximum 1 month per renewal per official source). Nationalities from worker-exporting countries (Bangladesh, India, Philippines, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Indonesia, etc.) must obtain pre-authorization. Work visa category listed at fee of 2,000,000 LBP. Domestic workers have a separate category with specific conditions.

visa_types.name: Domestic Worker / Household Staff Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals employed as domestic workers (maids, caregivers, cleaners) in Lebanese households.
visa_types.notes: Subject to specific conditions and prior authorization. Nationalities include workers from South/Southeast Asia (Philippines, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Indonesia, etc.) and parts of Africa. Employee must leave Lebanon with their employer or following end of contract. Tied to employer sponsorship.

visa_types.name: Artist / Performer Visa
visa_types.purpose: For entertainers, artists, models, nightclub performers, and spa/massage workers performing or contracted in Lebanon.
visa_types.notes: Issued by the Department of Artists at General Security under special conditions. Requires prior General Security authorization regardless of nationality. Female citizens ages 17-45 classified as artists or masseuses from certain countries (Eastern Europe, Central Asia, some East Asian nations) face additional scrutiny and mandatory pre-authorization even if their nationality is otherwise visa-on-arrival eligible. Specific categories include nightclub performers, models, and spa/massage workers.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled in or accepted to educational institutions in Lebanon.
visa_types.notes: Arab students receive a free 3-month student visa upon presenting a valid student card or admission certificate from an accredited Lebanese institution. Non-Arab students follow their nationality's standard tourist/entry visa process then convert to a residency permit. Visa listed as a distinct sub-category under visit visas (post/159). Religious students, volunteers, missionaries, and clergy have a separate sub-category.

visa_types.name: Religious / Missionary / Volunteer Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign clergy, religious students, volunteers, and missionaries serving or studying in Lebanon.
visa_types.notes: Explicitly listed as a separate visa sub-category on the General Security site. Foreigners affiliated with the Vatican have a specific track. Requires documentation of affiliation with a recognized religious or humanitarian organization registered in Lebanon.

visa_types.name: Medical / Sanitary Services Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Lebanon to receive medical treatment or access sanitary services.
visa_types.notes: Explicitly listed as a visit visa sub-category on general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/159. Applicant must present documentation from a Lebanese medical facility confirming appointment or treatment plan. Conditions (duration, fee) follow standard visit visa rules for the applicant's nationality.

visa_types.name: Family Visit / Reunion Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Lebanon to visit family members or for family reunification purposes.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct sub-category under visit visas (friendship/relationship visits and family reasons). Marriage certificates must conform to current Lebanese regulations for spousal applications. Spouse of a Lebanese national may qualify for extended stay. GCC nationals' domestic workers (maids, drivers, bodyguards) accompanying their employer-family are covered under employer's GCC entry.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: For holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports accredited to Lebanon or transiting on official duty.
visa_types.notes: Free multiple-entry 6-month visa issued at airport/border to all countries' diplomats, UN personnel, and DCAF workers. Many bilateral diplomatic passport waiver agreements exist (Armenia, Brazil, Colombia, Cyprus, Greece, Hungary, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Liechtenstein, Malta, Switzerland, Venezuela) granting full visa-free entry. Domestic workers of accredited diplomats are also covered.

visa_types.name: Investor Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals making a qualifying investment in Lebanon seeking annual residence.
visa_types.notes: Requires a blocked bank account of at least 150,000,000 LBP at a Lebanese bank (3-month statement, bank-signed), lease contract or title deed, investment contracts, commercial register extract, and professional license. Annual renewal required. Mandatory health insurance for foreigners. Path to permanent residence or citizenship is not officially documented.

visa_types.name: Converting Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals already in Lebanon seeking to change their visa/entry status to a different category (e.g., tourist to work).
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct visa category on general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/37 with a standard fee of 2,000,000 LBP. Allows change of status within Lebanon without exiting. Specific eligible conversions and conditions are not detailed on the publicly available official page.

visa_types.name: Renewing Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals extending their current visa or stay authorization while already in Lebanon.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct category on general-security.gov.lb/en/posts/37 with a fee of 2,000,000 LBP. Tourist/visit visas (initially 1 month) are typically extended free of charge up to 3 months at General Security offices. Extensions beyond 3 months are subject to approval and may require additional documentation. Visa validity cannot exceed the holder's passport validity.

visa_types.name: Courtesy Visa
visa_types.purpose: For VIPs and dignitaries entering Lebanon on a complimentary basis at the discretion of Lebanese authorities.
visa_types.notes: Granted at the discretion of the General Director of General Security or through Lebanese embassies/delegations abroad. Reserved for VIPs. No publicly published criteria or application process.

outbound_official.note: No accessible official Lebanese government outbound visa-list page located. Lebanon's General Security (general-security.gov.lb) and MFA (mfa.gov.lb) only publish inbound visa policy (who may enter Lebanon). The Lebanese Embassy in Jakarta maintains a page titled 'Visa Free for Lebanese citizens' at http://www.jakarta.mfa.gov.lb/visa-free-for-lebanese-citizens/visa-free-for-lebanese (an official gov.lb subdomain), but that URL is inaccessible (consistent connection timeouts) and its content cannot be verified. No centrally authoritative, accessible Lebanese government page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin was found. Rely on destination-side data.
